content: Legal (external counsel) dropped revised privacy policy + DPA template in Notion on Monday. 47 pages total. Anya flagged three sections needed Sebastien sign-off before customer rollout.
summary: Legal delivered privacy policy & DPA template

content: SOC2 audit requires written DPA covering data handling, sub-processor disclosure, breach notification SLAs, and PII retention limits. Non-negotiable for Wayne Industries contract.
summary: SOC2 mandates DPA with PII & breach clauses

content: Process: Legal drafts → Anya + Sebastien review → Priya notifies relevant customers of DPA availability → CS (Marcus) tracks signature + storage in Linear. Async review window: 5 business days max.
Sebastien blocked 1 hour Wed morning to review DPA language. Asked why GDPR Section 7 didn't explicitly mention Anthropic as sub-processor. Valid—fixed it same day.
GDPR compliance requires explicit customer consent for cross-border data transfer to AWS US regions. Voyage AI embedding calls also need processor agreement. Updated policy reflects both.
Anya caught that initial DPA had 30-day breach notification window. Acme Corp's security team wants 24-hour SLA. Negotiated middle ground: 24 hours to acknowledge, 72 hours to full disclosure.
summary: Acme forced tighter breach notification terms

content: PII data handling policy: customer data encrypted at rest (Cloudflare + AWS KMS), TLS 1.3 in transit. Dev team (Ari, Devon) signs off on control implementation. Annual audit via DataDog/Sentry logs.
summary: Encryption & monitoring standards for customer PII

content: I've now documented three policy rollouts (usage T&Cs, privacy, DPA). Pattern: legal always underestimates customer friction. Build 2-week buffer into timelines, not 1 week.
summary: Legal rollouts need 2-week customer negotiation slack

content: Priya sent DPA + privacy policy to 12 current customers + 4 warm prospects on Feb 24. Marcus set up tracking sheet. By Friday, Acme + Initech signed. Wayne Industries requested 3 amendments.
DPA signature storage: signed PDFs go to 1Password vault (backup in Notion page w/ signature date + customer name). Marcus responsible for 30-day follow-up on unsigned. Quarterly audit of coverage.

Wayne Industries legal team redlined DPA hard on data retention + deletion timelines. Wanted 90-day deletion SLA instead of 1 year. Escalated to Sebastien; he approved 6-month compromise to close deal.
summary: Wayne Industries negotiated shorter data retention

content: GDPR Article 28 requires processor (Clude) to document sub-processors in writing. Current list: Anthropic (LLM), Voyage AI (embeddings), AWS (infra), Snowflake (analytics). Updates require 30-day customer notice.
Globex Corp asked if we retain training data from their conversations. Checked w/ Ari: no—model inference only, logs deleted after 90 days. Updated DPA language to reflect. Customer signed by EOD.

Clude's policy: no PII used for model training or internal analytics. Data segregated by customer in Snowflake. Cloudflare WAF + Sentry alerting flag suspicious access patterns. Quarterly penetration test via third party.
